Models¶

CoTracker models:

Model Utils¶

cotracker.models.core.model_utils.bilinear_sampler(input, coords, align_corners=True, padding_mode='border')¶

Sample a tensor using bilinear interpolation

bilinear_sampler(input, coords) samples a tensor input at coordinates coords using bilinear interpolation. It is the same as torch.nn.functional.grid_sample() but with a different coordinate convention.

The input tensor is assumed to be of shape \((B, C, H, W)\), where \(B\) is the batch size, \(C\) is the number of channels, \(H\) is the height of the image, and \(W\) is the width of the image. The tensor coords of shape \((B, H_o, W_o, 2)\) is interpreted as an array of 2D point coordinates \((x_i,y_i)\).

Alternatively, the input tensor can be of size \((B, C, T, H, W)\), in which case sample points are triplets \((t_i,x_i,y_i)\). Note that in this case the order of the components is slightly different from grid_sample(), which would expect \((x_i,y_i,t_i)\).

If align_corners is True, the coordinate \(x\) is assumed to be in the range \([0,W-1]\), with 0 corresponding to the center of the left-most image pixel \(W-1\) to the center of the right-most pixel.

If align_corners is False, the coordinate \(x\) is assumed to be in the range \([0,W]\), with 0 corresponding to the left edge of the left-most pixel \(W\) to the right edge of the right-most pixel.

Similar conventions apply to the \(y\) for the range \([0,H-1]\) and \([0,H]\) and to \(t\) for the range \([0,T-1]\) and \([0,T]\).

Parameters:

  • input (Tensor) – batch of input images.

  • coords (Tensor) – batch of coordinates.

  • align_corners (bool, optional) – Coordinate convention. Defaults to True.

  • padding_mode (str, optional) – Padding mode. Defaults to “border”.

Returns:

sampled points.

Return type:

Tensor

cotracker.models.core.model_utils.get_grid(height, width, shape=None, dtype='torch', device='cpu', align_corners=True, normalize=True)¶
cotracker.models.core.model_utils.get_points_on_a_grid(size, extent, center=None, device=device(type='cpu'))¶

Get a grid of points covering a rectangular region

get_points_on_a_grid(size, extent) generates a size by size grid fo points distributed to cover a rectangular area specified by extent.

The extent is a pair of integer \((H,W)\) specifying the height and width of the rectangle.

Optionally, the center can be specified as a pair \((c_y,c_x)\) specifying the vertical and horizontal center coordinates. The center defaults to the middle of the extent.

Points are distributed uniformly within the rectangle leaving a margin \(m=W/64\) from the border.

It returns a \((1, \text{size} \times \text{size}, 2)\) tensor of points \(P_{ij}=(x_i, y_i)\) where

\[P_{ij} = \left( c_x + m -\frac{W}{2} + \frac{W - 2m}{\text{size} - 1}\, j,~ c_y + m -\frac{H}{2} + \frac{H - 2m}{\text{size} - 1}\, i \right)\]

Points are returned in row-major order.

Parameters:

  • size (int) – grid size.

  • extent (tuple) – height and with of the grid extent.

  • center (tuple, optional) – grid center.

  • device (str, optional) – Defaults to “cpu”.

Returns:

grid.

Return type:

Tensor

cotracker.models.core.model_utils.get_sift_sampled_pts(video, size, num_frames, extent, device=device(type='cpu'), num_sampled_frames=8, sampling_length_percent=0.25)¶
Parameters:

  • size (int)

  • num_frames (int)

  • extent (Tuple[float, ...])

  • device (device | None)

  • num_sampled_frames (int)

  • sampling_length_percent (float)

cotracker.models.core.model_utils.get_superpoint_sampled_pts(video, size, num_frames, extent, device=device(type='cpu'))¶
Parameters:

  • size (int)

  • num_frames (int)

  • extent (Tuple[float, ...])

  • device (device | None)

cotracker.models.core.model_utils.get_uniformly_sampled_pts(size, num_frames, extent, device=device(type='cpu'))¶
Parameters:

  • size (int)

  • num_frames (int)

  • extent (Tuple[float, ...])

  • device (device | None)

cotracker.models.core.model_utils.reduce_masked_mean(input, mask, dim=None, keepdim=False)¶

Masked mean

reduce_masked_mean(x, mask) computes the mean of a tensor input over a mask mask, returning

\[\text{output} = \frac {\sum_{i=1}^N \text{input}_i \cdot \text{mask}_i} {\epsilon + \sum_{i=1}^N \text{mask}_i}\]

where \(N\) is the number of elements in input and mask, and \(\epsilon\) is a small constant to avoid division by zero.

reduced_masked_mean(x, mask, dim) computes the mean of a tensor input over a mask mask along a dimension dim. Optionally, the dimension can be kept in the output by setting keepdim to True. Tensor mask must be broadcastable to the same dimension as input.

The interface is similar to torch.mean().

Parameters:

  • inout (Tensor) – input tensor.

  • mask (Tensor) – mask.

  • dim (int, optional) – Dimension to sum over. Defaults to None.

  • keepdim (bool, optional) – Keep the summed dimension. Defaults to False.

Returns:

mean tensor.

Return type:

Tensor

cotracker.models.core.model_utils.round_to_multiple_of_4(n)¶
cotracker.models.core.model_utils.sample_features4d(input, coords)¶

Sample spatial features

sample_features4d(input, coords) samples the spatial features input represented by a 4D tensor \((B, C, H, W)\).

The field is sampled at coordinates coords using bilinear interpolation. coords is assumed to be of shape \((B, R, 3)\), where each sample has the format \((x_i, y_i)\). This uses the same convention as bilinear_sampler() with align_corners=True.

The output tensor has one feature per point, and has shape \((B, R, C)\).

Parameters:

  • input (Tensor) – spatial features.

  • coords (Tensor) – points.

Returns:

sampled features.

Return type:

Tensor

cotracker.models.core.model_utils.sample_features5d(input, coords)¶

Sample spatio-temporal features

sample_features5d(input, coords) works in the same way as sample_features4d() but for spatio-temporal features and points: input is a 5D tensor \((B, T, C, H, W)\), coords is a \((B, R1, R2, 3)\) tensor of spatio-temporal point \((t_i, x_i, y_i)\). The output tensor has shape \((B, R1, R2, C)\).

Parameters:

  • input (Tensor) – spatio-temporal features.

  • coords (Tensor) – spatio-temporal points.

Returns:

sampled features.

Return type:

Tensor
